name: Should I add FAQPage schema to every page for AI search, or only some of them?
description: Use FAQPage schema only on pages with real, visible Q&A content—ideally 3–10 strong questions that match the schema exactly. Applied selectively, it improves cl

mainEntity.name: Should I add FAQPage schema to every page for AI search, or only some of them?

acceptedAnswer.text:
FAQPage schema is best used on pages that already have clear, visible question-and-answer sections, not everywhere on your site.
Overusing it on thin or irrelevant pages can create noise and policy issues.

Key factors:
- Schema questions must match visible text word-for-word on the page.
- 3–10 strong, relevant FAQs per page is a practical sweet spot.
- Duplicating the same FAQ block across many pages weakens the signal.
- Google has reduced FAQ-rich result usage, but AI and search still use the clarity it provides.

Practically, pick a handful of high-intent pages (services, pricing, onboarding) and add a visible FAQ section, then mirror that with FAQPage JSON-LD.
Focus on questions your customers genuinely ask, especially around process, timing, and costs.
FAQ schema works best when it reflects real user uncertainty, not manufactured keyword lists.

short_answer:"Use FAQPage schema only on pages with real, visible Q&A content—ideally 3–10 strong questions that match the schema exactly. Applied selectively, it improves clarity for AI search without triggering duplication or quality issues."
acceptedAnswer.description: Use FAQPage schema only on pages with real, visible Q&A content—ideally 3–10 strong questions that match the schema exactly. Applied selectively, it improves clarity for AI search without triggering duplication or quality issues.
